meaning: int.[亦作 hurray] 好哇!n.欢呼声,激动,纷争v.(向...)欢呼
examples: The leap was so well taken that all the boys shouted, "Hurrah for Pinocchio!"
I haven't even begun to think about quitting, or having a last hurrah, or allowing my career to wind down.
The princess and the shadow went out on the balcony to show themselves, and get another hurrah!
Grease was the last hurrah for the musical, which critics had proclaimed dead 20 years before.
This increases the number of new things we have to play with and Hurrah!
